rulings.Case Title: FIRESIDE THRIFT CO. vs. MCDOWELL, CHRISTY A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Opposition To Claim Of Exemption Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 4, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FIRESIDE THRIFT CO.'S Opposition To Claim Of Exemption Hearing. Sherriff to withhold $100 per pay period. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA BANK & TRUST VS. KYLE T. REATH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Hearing For A Determination And Order On Third-Party Claims
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 4, 2015, Line 16, DEFENDANT LYNN KEPPEL'S Motion For Hearing For A Determination And Order On Third-Party Claims. The motion is granted in part. The funds transferred from Mr. Keppel's earnings into his daughter's account are subject to levy, however the remaining funds are exempt. Mr. Keppel has transferred $900 ($100 per month) since judgment was entered in December 2015. Therefore, all funds excluding $900 transferred since December 2015 are exempt. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO TIFFANY GARDENS HOMEOWNERS VS. TIFFANY GARDENS LP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 4, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT DRAKE GARDNER'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. The motion is denied. Defendant is not entitled to relief under CCP 473. The motion is untimely because it is outside the 6-month deadline and defendant failed to provide a proposed pleading. Defendant fails to demonstrate a basis for equitable relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN (BATROA) FOR THE et al VS. ROBERT CROSS AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions; Or In The Alternative For Evidentiary Sanctions, Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 4, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANTS ROBERT CROSS AND SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions; Or In The Alternative For Evidentiary Sanctions, Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion is GRANTED. Plaintiff Michael L. Levine shall appear for deposition on or before September 11, 2015. Should Plaintiff Michael L. Levine fail to appear for deposition on or before September 11, 2015, the Court will, upon proof of plaintiff's failure to appear, consider evidentiary, terminating and monetary sanctions. Defendants' request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $3,500. Costs associated with the pending deposition are not included in the award of sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBIN LEAMY et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 4, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that defendant (through Ms. Brown) made positive assertions that Plaintiff detrimentally relied upon. The cited deposition testimony does not conclusively show that plaintiff is merely relying upon implied representations to establish his claims. The demurrer is overruled as to the negligence cause of action. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to create a duty of care under Alvarez v. BAC Home Loans Servicing, L.P. (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 941, 945. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: PETER M OWENS VS. IRIS CANADA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Peter Owens, Carolyn Radisch And Stephen Owens' Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Third Demand For Inspection Of Real Property And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 4, 2015, Line 3, Plaintiffs Peter Owens, Carolyn Radisch And Stephen Owens' Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs' Third Demand For Inspection Of Real Property And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' Motion to Compel is GRANTED. Plaintiffs' Third Demand for Inspection of Real Property, Set One, shall take place on a date to be determined at hearing on the matter. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,600. Prevailing party to provide a proposed Order at the time of hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JENI YANG VS. LEVI STRAUSS & CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Notice Requiring (I) Identification Of Potential Witnesses And (2) Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 4, 2015, Line 15, PLAINTIFF JENI YANG'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Notice Requiring (I) Identification Of Potential Witnesses And (2) Production Of Documents. 1. DEFENDANT SHALL DISCLOSE THE WORK ADDRESSES AND TELEPHONE NUMBERS OF CURRENT EMPLOYEES PREVIOUSLY IDENTIFIED; 2. DEFENDANT SHALL NOTIFY CURRENT EMPLOYEES PREVIOUSLY IDENTIFIED THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS THEIR HOME ADDRESSES AND TELEPHONE NUMBERS AND SHALL AFFORD SUCH EMPLOYEES WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO OBJECT IN WRITING TO DISCLOSURE THEREOF; 3. DEFENDANT SHALL NOTIFY FORMER EMPLOYEES PREVIOUSLY IDENTIFIED THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS THEIR HOME AND WORK ADDRESSES AND TELEPHONE NUMBERS AND SHALL AFFORD SUCH FORMER EMPLOYEES WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO OBJECT IN WRITING TO DISCLOSURE THEREOF; 4. DEFENDANT SHALL NOTIFY JOB APPLICANTS PREVIOUSLY IDENTIFIED THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS THEIR HOME AND WORK AND TELEPHONE NUMBERS AND SHALL AFFORD SUCH JOB APPLICANTS WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO OBJECT IN WRITING TO DISCLOSURE THEREOF; 5. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER AS TO THE FORM OF NOTIFICATION TO BE USED FOR COMPLIANCE WITH PARAGRAPHS 1 THROUGH 4 ABOVE; 6. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE WITNESS IDENTIFICATION INFORMATION IN THE CATEGORIES SET FORTH ABOVE EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT THAT THE WITNESSES IN QUESTION HAVE OBJECTED TO SUCH DISCLOSURE; 7. DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE A FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSE THAT IT HAS NO RECORDS OF PRIOR COMPLAINTS OF PREGNANCY DISCRIMINATION OR SEX DISCRIMINATION BASED ON PREGNANCY FOR THE LAST TEN YEARS; 8. BOTH PARTIES' REQUESTS FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
